channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Gas explosion at Chinese coal mine kills at least 90
item.description: President Xi Jinping has called on authorities nationwide to learn from the incident.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Which star players will miss the tournament?
item.description: Al Jazeera takes a look at the high-profile players who will play no part in the tournament in North America.

item.title: Iran war day 85: Tehran says major gaps remain in US talks
item.description: Pakistan army chief Asim Munir arrives in Tehran as mediation efforts to secure a US-Iran peace deal intensify.

item.title: Israeli forces attack central Gaza despite ceasefire agreement
item.description: Video shows the moment Israeli forces struck Nuseirat and Bureij refugee camps in central Gaza, injuring dozens.

item.title: Gilgeous-Alexander leads Thunder past Spurs for 2-1 NBA playoff lead
item.description: Reigning NBA champions see four members of their bench finish in double figures to set up the precious road win.

item.title: Burn-like rashes and hunger: Gaza’s children face skin disease crisis
item.description: Overcrowded camps in Gaza see a surge in skin diseases. Children are most affected as healthcare services collapse.

item.title: Trump releases new batch of previously classified UFO files
item.description: The Trump administration has released a second batch of previously classified files on UFO sightings.

item.title: Over 1.6 million pilgrims arrive in Saudi Arabia for hajj pilgrimage
item.description: More than 1.6 million Muslim pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage

item.title: Are India and Pakistan quietly preparing to restart dialogue?
item.description: India and Pakistan remain publicly entrenched, even as unofficial voices push for renewed dialogue and restraint.

item.title: Why are anti-migrant attacks increasing in South Africa?
item.description: Rising xenophobia in South Africa leaves migrants facing harassment, job losses and community violence.

item.title: Some fear US-Philippines war games are drawing conflict closer to home
item.description: Critics of US, Philippine military cooperation say closely aligning with Washington makes Manila a possible target.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump’s top US intelligence official
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard has resigned as US Director of National Intelligence, citing her husband’s cancer diagnosis.

item.title: Ebola outbreak disrupts life along the DRC-Uganda border
item.description: With almost 750 suspected Ebola cases in the DRC, health measures intensified along the DRC–Uganda border

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran says diplomacy continues but no deal yet with US
item.description: Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed at least 3,111 people since March 2, with 9,432 injured: Health Ministry.

item.title: Israeli blockade traps would-be Hajj pilgrim in Gaza
item.description: For years, 64-year-old Najia Abu Lehia dreamed of performing Hajj with her husband.

item.title: Premier League finale: Arsenal, relegation, Champions League, Salah and Pep
item.description: Arsenal may have won the title, but the Premier League story is far from over with European spots and survival in play.

item.title: ‘Everest Man’ breaks his own record after historic 32nd climb
item.description: Legendary mountaineer Kami Rita Sherpa just broke his own record after climbing Mount Everest for the 32nd time.

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil to appeal US deportation case to Supreme Court
item.description: Pro-Palestine advocate takes claim that Trump administration targeted him for protected free speech to top US court.

item.title: US judge dismisses indictment against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: Judge says the human smuggling probe was reopened after Salvadoran national filed his lawsuit against his deportation.

item.title: Bolivia in crisis: Social unrest, demands for president to resign escalate
item.description: Protests have spiraled out of control and President Rodrigo Paz is facing demands to resign.

item.title: Man City boss and Palestine advocate Pep Guardiola to step down
item.description: Man City manager Pep Guardiola to step down as manager of Manchester City after a trophy-laden 10-year reign.

item.title: San Diego’s Muslims, a mosque, and a city shaken
item.description: After a deadly shooting, San Diego’s Muslim community mourns, supports one another, and searches for healing.

item.title: What are the implications of US sanctions in Lebanon?
item.description: Nine people targeted, including Lebanese military officers.

item.title: Thousands in Cuba rally for Raul Castro to protest US indictment
item.description: Thousands of people are protesting outside the US embassy in Havana to show their support for Raul Castro.

item.title: Slovenia’s parliament approves right-wing Janez Jansa as prime minister
item.description: Approval of populist former leader is a shift for the EU country recently run by a liberal government.
